items.claim: Alessandro Costa has a superior striking ability compared to Cody Durden.
items.quote: I think Alessandro Costa is too good at striking.

items.claim: Alessandro Costa possesses a significant jiu-jitsu advantage over Cody Durden.
items.quote: I think that the jiu-jitsu advantage for Costa is real

items.claim: Cody Durden has a history of gassing out in later rounds.
items.quote: he was gassing out when I saw him getting to the third round

items.claim: Ryan Gandre's tendency to storm in with power shots without defensive hand positioning makes him vulnerable to being tagged.
items.quote: When he throws these big bombs though, he's coming storming in... I think he will get tagged at some point.

items.claim: John Garza has poor cardio, evidenced by his performance in the third round against Nick Gelaj.
items.quote: He was gassing out when I saw him getting to the third round against Nick Gelaj.

items.claim: Cesar Almeida has a more dynamic striking game and more experience than Damir Pinas.
items.quote: Cesar Almeida's got a much more dynamic striking game, more experience.

items.claim: Damir Pinas tends to tire out in the later rounds of a fight.
items.quote: win rounds two and three as Pinales gets tired

items.claim: Tracy Cortez is closer in striking skill to Kong Wang than Kong Wang is in grappling skill to Tracy Cortez.
items.quote: I do think she's closer in the striking department than Kong Wang is in the grappling department.

items.claim: Luke Riley possesses more power and snap in his strikes than Kai Kamaka.
items.quote: Luke Riley's got pop on his shots. Luke Riley's got a little bit of snap when he hits you.

items.claim: Kai Kamaka lacks the offensive danger or consequence in his strikes to threaten Luke Riley.
items.quote: Kai Kamaka very technically sound, but not dangerous... Kai Kamaka just not consequential enough for me to pick him
